problem_addressed: Creating embodied agents that can continuously explore, plan, and develop new skills in open-ended worlds without human intervention.
core_claim: VOYAGER is the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent that can continuously explore the world, acquire diverse skills, and make novel discoveries without human intervention in Minecraft.
key_contributions: An automatic curriculum that maximizes exploration
An ever-growing skill library of executable code for storing and retrieving complex behaviors
A new iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement
novelty: Unlike prior works that use hierarchical reinforcement learning or LLMs with limited exploration flexibility, VOYAGER employs an automatic curriculum that unfolds in a bottom-up manner driven by curiosity, enabling truly open-ended exploration, and uniquely integrates a skill library for developing complex behaviors crucial for lifelong learning.
method_summary: VOYAGER interacts with GPT-4 via blackbox queries to generate executable code for Minecraft actions, consisting of three components: an automatic curriculum proposing tasks based on exploration progress, a skill library storing and retrieving complex behaviors, and an iterative prompting mechanism refining programs using environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification.
method_components: Skill library for storing and retrieving complex behaviors
Iterative prompting mechanism using code as action space
key_innovation: The iterative prompting mechanism that incorporates environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification for program improvement, with self-verification being most critical as it determines when to move to new tasks or reattempt unsuccessful ones.
assumptions: GPT-4 can generate high-quality executable code for Minecraft actions
The Minecraft environment provides sufficient feedback signals for learning
The skill library can effectively store and retrieve complex behaviors
The automatic curriculum can propose achievable tasks that drive exploration
theoretical_grounding: The paper relies on empirical evaluation in Minecraft, showing VOYAGER outperforms baselines in unique items discovered (3.3× more), distances traveled (2.3× longer), and tech tree milestones unlocked (up to 15.3× faster), with ablation studies confirming component importance.

hyperparameters: Warm-up schedule to gradually incorporate agent's state and additional context based on number of completed tasks

stated_limitations: Relies on GPT-4 via blackbox queries which may be expensive and have rate limits
Works specifically in Minecraft environment which may not generalize to other domains
Limited to the capabilities of GPT-4 without fine-tuning
unstated_weaknesses: No mention of how the agent handles uncertainty or calibration
Approach seems computationally intensive due to continuous exploration
No discussion of performance with limited data or noisy inputs
threats_to_validity: Domain mismatch: Minecraft vs. medical tabular data
Generalization concerns: Skills learned in Minecraft may not transfer to medical data
Potential overfitting to the Minecraft environment
Limited evaluation of performance on tasks outside Minecraft domain
open_questions: How can the approach be adapted for non-embodied domains like medical tabular data?
What are the computational costs and resource requirements?
How does the agent handle noisy or uncertain information?
Can the skills learned in Minecraft be transferred to other domains?
future_work: Extending the approach to other environments beyond Minecraft
Improving the efficiency of the learning process
Developing methods for transferring skills across domains
Enhancing the agent's ability to handle uncertainty and noise
gaps_this_enables: Methods for lifelong learning in non-embodied domains
Techniques for adapting embodied learning approaches to tabular data
Approaches for handling noisy labels in small datasets
Methods for calibration-aware learning in medical applications
relevance_to_topic: The paper introduces novel approaches to lifelong learning and skill acquisition that could potentially inspire methods for handling noisy labels in small medical tabular datasets, though it's primarily focused on embodied agents in Minecraft.

title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
